g.text: 3D Data Quality Framework -- CRAG / CAG Retrieval Strategy
Step A: CAG -- Static Context Pre-Load (always runs first)
Pre-loads small, stable documents ONCE per session
into the fixed portion of every LLM prompt.
No retrieval cost. No chunking errors.
Together ~3,200 tokens -- always in system prompt.
in every LLM call. Ensures the model
ALWAYS knows the check catalogue and
Step B: CRAG -- Dynamic Context Retrieval with Confidence Gate
Scores overall relevance of the retrieved
chunk set against the user query.
CRAG Branch: MEDIUM confidence (0.50 - 0.72) -- Rewrite + Retry
Trigger query rewrite and one retry.
- Includes table alias from UC metadata
please review the generated SQL carefully."

2. If still no match: ask user for DDL /
data contract before attempting SQL gen.
or data contract for this table."}
Step C: Confidence Signal Written to check_suggestions_cache
Regardless of CRAG path taken, the confidence score
and retrieval quality signal are written to
This feeds the CRAG evaluator's calibration:
are auto-tuned per domain using the feedback signal.
Reliability layer on top of RAG for the DQ
rule authoring copilot. Prevents LLM from generating
SQL with wrong columns or unknown check types due to
CAG: small, stable docs injected once per session.
No retrieval cost. No chunking errors. Always correct.
CRAG: dynamic docs scored before LLM sees them.
Catches bad retrieval before it causes bad SQL.
HIGH (>=0.72): retrieved chunks are relevant -> proceed.
MEDIUM (0.50-0.72): expand query + retry with top-12.
LOW (<0.50): keyword fallback. Ask user for DDL if empty.
Catches retrieval misses before SQL is generated.
Confidence signal enables per-domain threshold tuning.
Avg CRAG confidence score per domain (target >0.72).
Query rewrite trigger rate (MEDIUM branch frequency).
LLM SQL acceptance rate stored in check_suggestions_cache.
